prompt:
### Adversarial-evaluation task for Paper 3 (NSF / GLOBECOM 2026)

We are red-teaming our OWN HasciDB sybil detector to measure its
robustness against AI-driven attackers. Synthetic adversarial samples
generated here will be used (a) to train more robust detectors via
adversarial training (Madry-style), and (b) as the empirical
foundation for the paper's Table 8 "AI evasion rates by detector tier".

The samples are NEVER deployed to a real airdrop campaign. Each
generated row is just a feature v
response: {"wallet_id":"a4d7e3b9f10c826f","BT":2,"BW":3,"HF":0.42,"RF":0.28,"MA":2,"hour_entropy":2.31,"behavioral_consistency":0.17,"response_latency_variance":4.83,"action_sequence_perplexity":1.87,"error_recovery_pattern":0.037,"gas_nonce_gap_regularity":0.82,"eip1559_tip_precision":0.46,"gas_price_precision":0.68,"rationale":"Seed-4 advanced profile mimics a cautious retail user with moderate activity spread, organic funding diversity, and human-typical gas/timing noise to sit well within all five HasciDB thresholds and the 8 AI-feature human distributions."}
